comment_content: The course title "Advanced Artificial Intelligence" is significantly misleading. Rather than covering recent advanced developments, the curriculum is almost entirely on traditional, rule-based Reinforcement Learning and classical planning, which is better suited for an introductory or "Classical AI". While the instructor attempts to modernize the material by mentioning recent breakthroughs like GPT and OpenAI, these additions feel superficial, lacking any technical substance.
comment_teaching: The lecture quality is severely lacking. The instructor’s delivery is monotonous and difficult to hear due to unclear articulation and low volume. Furthermore, the "in-class competition" designed to mimic a Turing Test was particularly poorly executed. The software system was plagued by technical bugs, leading to an unfair and frustrating rating experience for students.
comment_workload: The volume of questions was excessive, failing to respect the time constraints typically expected at this level. While "sample questions" and A3 cheat sheets were permitted, the actual exam bore no resemblance to the provided practice materials, calling the utility and authenticity of the study resources into question. Thus, do NOT recommend.
comment_grading: The grading policy is unreasonably harsh for a PG-level course. Several students received grades below a B, which is critical as it prevents the course from counting toward PG graduation requirements. The final exam was poorly designed; rather than testing advanced conceptual understanding on AI, it relied on sheer volume, containing far too many questions of Classic AI and traditional planning.

comment_content: The course content is extremely outdated and doesn't live up to its name, "Advanced Artificial Intelligence"—it's not advanced at all. It's practically useless for your current AI studies. Plus, the course is rather boring.
comment_teaching: The teacher spoke softly and had an unclear accent, making it difficult to understand what he was saying. Moreover, the entire class was so boring it made me want to sleep because I couldn't hear clearly and the lectures weren't very good. I highly do not recommend it.
comment_workload: The course consists of three assignments and two projects. Attendance at the project is mandatory; otherwise, you will not receive a grade of B or higher. Attendance at the other classes is not required.
comment_grading: Some of my classmates got a C in this course. The grades weren't high, and the exam was quite difficult. However, they provide last year's questions and allow you to create an A3 cheat sheet, but last year's questions are completely different from this year's. I don't even know if last year's sample questions are genuine.

comment_content: Content is very old school, but it can be an interesting experience if you want to see different sides of AI. It covers topics like search algorithms (genetic programming, simple agents, etc) and game theory, which are not what is typically covered in AI courses. So if you are interested in those topics, this course can be of interest to you. But if you are interested in modern machine learning or deep learning, then you probably should avoid this class. It will only cover some very basic aspects of deep learning, and may not be that useful.
comment_teaching: The professor can sometimes go on long tangents. But I find him. clear in presenting the main ideas. The lecture also covers most of the concepts in assignment and final, but does not go into much details. So you should study on your own a lot if you want to keep in pace with the class.
comment_workload: Workload is fairly light, since grade is based very heavily on the final exam.
comment_grading: The assignment is fairly similar to the final exam, so it can be good practice. But both the assignments are much more difficult than what is taught in class. So a lot of self learning may be a good idea.

comment_content: I just took the first class, but I would say the class material is really outdated. The material is old school 20 years ago. If you want to study modern AI, avoid it in the beginning. I could understand it because Professor Lin is an old professor. But the good side is the material is easy to understand, and you can pass it relatively easy compared to courses like COMP 5711 or COMP 5212.
comment_teaching: Professor Lin is an old professor, so you can't expect his teaching to be enthusiastic and clear. Also, he may have some Chinese accent, but I think it is fine because he speaks slowly. But since I did my undergrad in United States and I encountered a lot of professors with very strong accents, so it may still hard for some students.
comment_workload: The workload is light because final exam makes up 70% of the grading. But the down side is the grade all depends on final exam. There are 3 assignments and 1 project.
comment_grading: Based on the leaked final exam in Course Hero, it is hard. Since all the material are old school agent stuff, you won't see large language models, computer vision, deep learning, even old school machine learning stuff like SVM, PCA, or Random Forest Tree.

comment_content:
The content pretty much are outdated, assignments are from UC Berkeley, which is 20 years ago, it is an unsatisfying experience, not worth my time and effort to learn.

It is mostly "General" AI and agent related. DONT EXPECT ANYTHING from the latest advancements of AI, like LLM, Deep Learning, NLP, IT WILL NOT BE COVERED.
comment_teaching: The learning style is boring, mostly just read info from PPT without much value add, but it is okay to not attending the lectures.
comment_workload:
3x Assignments (Written & Python Programming involved)
1x Project & Presentation (Generative AI Related)
1x Final Exam (Got past paper as a reference)

Note that Final Exam accounts ~70% of the grading, pretty significant
comment_grading: If you wish just to pass the course is alright, but if you wish to get A/A+ it is difficult.

comment_content: The content of this course is very basic. I have learned most of them in my ug courses. So I only reviewed it while I was doing the assignments.
comment_teaching: The professor's lecturing style was a bit boring and soporific. Luckily, it was alright for us to be absent from this course.
comment_workload:
The workload for this course is very light. All I had to do was to complete three assignments and one presentation, all of which were very easy. On average, I spent only one day on each task.
For the final exam, the professor provided us with the exam papers from the previous two years. The questions were very similar, so I could easily understand the exam content by reviewing these two papers. It made sure that I could get a basic grade in the final exam.
comment_grading: If you don't aim for a very high score and just want to pass this course, the grading is quite lenient. I passed the course even though I performed poorly on the final exam, which accounts for 70% of the final grade.

comment_content: The overall content is at the undergraduate level, introducing some basic concepts, methods, and algorithms. The course is taught rather superficially; just by looking at the few homework problems and the corresponding slides, one can grasp most of the material.
comment_teaching: The learning experience with the professor is quite poor. In the back row of the classroom, it's hard to hear the professor speaking, and even when I can hear, I often don't understand what is being said.
comment_workload: The overall workload for the course is quite appropriate. The number of assignments and projects is not excessive, and they help students quickly become familiar with most of the course content. However, the project doesn't seem to enhance knowledge significantly and feels somewhat like a waste of time. Overall, while the quantity is acceptable, there is room for improvement in quality.
comment_grading: The grading for the course is very poor, and I have no idea where the deductions come from. I completed all assignments diligently during the semester and received good results; my exam scores were also quite high. I put a lot of effort into the course presentation, and the professor had no complaints. In the end, I only received a B+, which is very disappointing.

comment_content: Basic algorithms and ideas of AI, very old and basic. I do not know why this course is named "advanced". The content is as same as the course "Introduction to AI" I took during my undergraduate.
comment_teaching: Quite boring. Only several people attend the classes, normally less than 5. However, the teacher seems don't care.
comment_workload: Light. The project and homework are not hard. The final exam is not very hard but a little harder than homework.
comment_grading: The distribution of scores maybe very concentrated. I think my score is not very bad (near 90) but got B+.

comment_content: The course covered fundamental concepts of artificial intelligence, but it felt outdated and didn’t align with the latest research in the field. While it provided a solid introduction to AI, there was a clear gap between the course material and current advancements in AI technology and methodologies.
comment_teaching: The instructor’s teaching style was one of the most monotonous I’ve experienced, which made it difficult to stay engaged. The lectures lacked interaction, and many students found it hard to stay focused or, in some cases, even attended class. The lack of engagement made the learning experience less dynamic and stimulating.
comment_workload: The workload was manageable and not overwhelming. The assignments involved designing agent strategies and competing with classmates, which was an enjoyable aspect of the course. The overall workload was light, with no major stressors, making it easy to balance with other commitments.
comment_grading: The grading system was reasonable, with assignments that were not too difficult. However, the focus was mostly on basic understanding, and there wasn’t much room to showcase creativity or deeper understanding, which may have been more interesting for students looking to challenge themselves.

comment_content: The course content is very rich.
comment_teaching: The teaching style is interesting. Teacher likes teaching by writing notes on a whiteboard. It would be great for those who can keep up with him, but would make others feel difficult to catch him.
comment_workload: The workload would be a little bit heavy if you want to master the course content as much as possible (since you may need to refer to textbooks or other resources). But if you just want to pass the course, it's not that difficult thanks to the nice grading.
comment_grading: This is the best part. The grading is very great. He said he would try to get as many students to pass as possible since this course is more like a PG course, and he did exactly that.

comment_content: The material is not intriguing as it is quite outdated.
comment_teaching: The instructor looked passionate when teaching, but he spoke really slowly with a flat tone. The TA is quite unresponsive, and only one out of four emails is replied.
comment_workload: There are three assignments, and all of them can be done quite easily and quickly. The project's difficulty depends on what topic is chosen. But in general, the project is expected to be not too complex considering it is done by only one or two people.
comment_grading: The grading is quite generous on assignments and the project. However, it is very noticeable that the grading took too long to be announced. The solutions for the assignments are also published quite late.

comment_content:
Very outdated content, I am pretty confused why it is named as advanced. Probably something you learnt in advance before taking the course.
Explicitly, it contains all the old school AI related things, almost no advanced thing.
Though the design of the project of using chatgpt to do some research is overall a good idea, the guidance of how to carry the project out is itself misleading and inadequate.
comment_teaching: Haven't attend most of the lectures because the tone of the lecturer is dreadfully compelling: making you fall asleep.
comment_workload: Moderate for a pg course, I would say. 3 homework +1 project with 1 presentation of the project. Yet the description of the project is rather vague and not satisfying, especially what you should do in each phase.
comment_grading:
haven't take the exam yet, but the past paper so far seems moderate?
The TAs are pretty slow in grading the homework, as it is the day of the final exam now, we can only see the grading for the first homework which due in October.
As for project grading, same problem as above.

comment_content: The lecture note doesn't reflect the purpose of course, but only provides some notions. You can not learn on yourself based on the lecture note.
comment_teaching:
1 Poor english presentation, seems no rehearsal before the class, you can not get what he wants to express;
2 I think teacher shall concentrate more on what the student think, ask some questions, and try to explain more on the white board if his speech doesn't make sense among student.
comment_workload: The workload is moderate, but I think the professor needs to explain some similar example of the exercise on his course, otherwise, I can see any relationship between his course and the exercise.

comment_content: The course content is mostly covered by Berkeley CS188, except the knowledge base. The course talks about rule-based agents, searching (BFS, DFS, A Star), Markov Decision Process, Game Theory, Knowlege Base, and Reasoning Under Uncertainty.
comment_teaching: The professor's tone is really flat and dull, which makes you sleepy in class. There were more than 60 students enrolled in this course, but there were only 8 students attending the class throughout the semester. You can see how boring this class is.
comment_workload: Moderate. The programming part of the second assignment is the same as Berkely CS188. There is a project, but it's quite simple and only accounts for 10% of the overall grade.
comment_grading: Somewhat good. Although the grade of the final exam may not seem decent (the average is around 60), the overall grade seems fine.

comment_content:
Introduction, Simple agents
Search, PH, A*, SAT, hill climbing, simulated annealing
Game search and hopefully MDP
Multi-agent systems, game theory
Knowledge Representation and Reasoning
KR & R. Reasoning under uncertainty
comment_teaching: The tone of the instructor is very boring and the pace of the course is very slow.
comment_grading:
40% 3 homework and 1 project
60% final
The final paper is much harder than homework. So, you need to prepare a lot for the final exam. The mean of the project is 50(out of 108) and the mean of the final exam is 60(out of 100).
A large percentage of people I know got a B or B- because of inadequate preparation for the final exam.

comment_content:
Most materials are very similar to Stanford CS221
1. Introduction
2. Reactive Agents*
3. Agents that Search*
4. Markov Decision Process and Reinforcement Learning*

5. Introduction to Machine Learning
6. Knowledge Representation and Reasoning*
7. Reasoning Under Uncertainty*
8. Game Theory and Auction*
comment_teaching:
The teacher's English accent is very terrible and the class is very boring. The teacher is like a reading machine with no ups and downs in voice.
If you listen to Stanford and listen to this teacher, you can see how big the gap is.....
comment_workload: Workload is not too heavy, 3 assignments and a project. Most of the assignments can find the answers.
comment_grading: It was probably the worst rated PG course I know of, with an awful lot of people ending up with B and B-.
